title: System and method for monitoring moisture content of products on a production line
abstract: The present invention relates to a method and system for identifying moisture content of produce and/or products on a production line.
claims: Claims 1. A method for inspecting an operational quality of a production line, the method comprising: imaging a product on a production line conveyor belt using at least one imaging sensor, wherein the at least one imaging sensor is positioned at or following a product preparation station, to enable imaging of the product after said comestible products exits the product preparation station, and wherein the imaging sensor is operative at a wavelength in the range between about 8 pm - about 14 pm; measuring a thermal radiation, in at least one image obtained from the imaging; computing the moisture content of the product based on the measured thermal radiation; and determining, based on the computed moisture content an operational property of the production line. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the product preparation station comprises a drying station, a coating station, a baking station, a heating station, a roasting station, or a frying station. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the imaging comprising obtaining a time stamp for each image captured. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, further comprising detecting an amount and/or spread of the product based on the at least one image and wherein determining the operational property of the production line is further based on the determined amount and/or spread of the product. 5. The method of claim 4, further comprising determining an operational property of the conveyor of the production line based thereon. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, further comprising detecting a size and/or shape of the produce, based on at least one image and wherein determining the operational property of the production line is further based on the determined size and/or shape of the product. 7. The method of claim 6, further comprising determining an operational property of an extruder of the production line based on the detected size and/or shape. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein computing the moisture content of the product comprises applying an artificial intelligence (Al) algorithm and/or big data analytics on at least one image. 9. The method according to any one of claims 1-8, further comprising issuing an alert if the at least one operational property deviates from a predefined range. 10. The method of claim any one of 1-9, wherein determining the operational property of the production line comprises applying an Al algorithm and/or big data analytics on a plurality of images. 11. The method according to any one of claims 1-10, further comprising computing a trend in a detected and/or predicted moisture content of the product based on the Al algorithm and/or big data analysis. 12. The method according to claim 11, wherein the trend is indicative of performance of the production line, a contamination, a deformation, incorrect texture, issue with an ingredient, uneven heating, fluctuation in heating, a decrease or increase in heating station temperature, increased or decreased moisture content of a heating station, humidity along the production line, or any combination thereof. 13. The method according to claim 11 or 12, wherein the Al algorithm is configured to conduct a root cause analysis a production line fault based on the trend. 14. The method according to claim 13, further comprising recommending one or more steps to be taken to correct the fault, based on the root cause analysis. 15. The method according to any one of claims 1-14, further comprising heating the product prior to imaging, wherein the heating comprises baking, cooking, roasting, drying, dehydrating, frying, glazing, coating, melting, extruding, or any combination thereof. 16. The method according to any of claims 1-15, wherein the method further comprises a second imaging by a second imaging sensor configured to image the comestible product at another station of the production line and/or at a different wavelength. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the other station is an extrusion station. 35 RECTIFIED SHEET (RULE 91) 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the different wavelength is in the visible spectrum or UV spectrum. 19. The method according to any of claims 1-18, wherein the product is selected from the group consisting of: a coating, a snack, a baked product, a glazed product, a salted food product, a dried food product, pharmaceutical product, cosmetic product, tobacco product, a powder, or any combination thereof. 20. The method according to any of claims 1-19, wherein computing the moisture content comprises identifying changes in the moisture percentage level of about 1% or less. 21. A moisture content inspecting system comprising: a production line comprising a product preparation station; a first imaging sensor operative at a wavelength in the range between about 8 pm - about 14 pm, wherein the first imaging sensor is positioned at or following the product preparation station, wherein the first imaging sensor is configured image a product conveyed on a conveyor belt of the production line after exiting the product preparation station; and a processor configured to: measure a thermal radiation in at least one image obtained from the imaging, compute the moisture content of the product based on the measured thermal radiation, and determine based on the computed moisture content, an operational property of the production line. 22. The system of claim 21, wherein the product preparation station comprises a drying station, a coating station, a baking station, a heating station, a roasting station, or a frying station. 23. The system of claim 21 or 22, wherein the imaging comprising obtaining a time stamp for each image captured. 24. The system of any one of claims 21-23, wherein the processor is further configured to detect amount and/or spread of the product and wherein determining the operational property of the production line is further based on the determined amount and/or spread of the product. 25. The system of any one of claims 21-24, wherein the operational property is a property of the conveyor belt of the production line based thereon. 26. The system of any one of claims 21-25, wherein the processor is further configured to measure a size and/or shape of the produce, based on at least one image, and wherein determining the operational property of the production line is further based on the determined size and/or shape of the product. 27. The system of claim 26, wherein the production line further comprises an extruder and wherein the processor is further configured to determine an operational property of the extruder based on the detected size and/or shape. 28. The system of any one of claims 21-27, wherein computing the moisture content of the product comprises applying an artificial intelligence (Al) algorithm and/or big data analytics on a plurality of images. 29. The system of any one of claim 21-28, wherein determining the operational property of the production line comprises applying an Al algorithm and/or big data analytics on a plurality of images. 30. The system according to any one of claims 21-29, wherein the processor is further configured to issue an alert if the computed moisture content deviates from a predefined range. 31. The system according to any one of claims 21-30, wherein the processor is configured to identify a trend in the at moisture content over time, wherein the trend in the moisture content is identified by the applying of the Al algorithm and/or the big data analysis. 32. The system according to any one of claims 21-31, wherein the system has a sensitivity to a change in the moisture percentage level of about 1% or less. 33. The system according to any one of claims 21-32, further comprising a second imaging sensor configured to image the comestible product at another station of the production line and/or at a different wavelength. 34. The system of claim 33, wherein the other station is an extrusion station. 35. The system of claim 33, wherein the different wavelength is a wavelength in the visible or UV spectrum. 36. A method for inspecting an amount size and/or shape of a comestible product on a production line, the method comprising: imaging the comestible product using at least one imaging sensor, wherein the at least one imaging sensor is positioned prior to a packaging station of the production line and is configured to enable imaging of the comestible product while it is being conveyed on a conveyor belt of the production line, and wherein the imaging sensor is operative at a wavelength in the range between about 0.01 pm - about 14 pm; determining an amount, size and/or shape of the comestible product based on the imaging; and determining, based on the determined amount, size and/or shape, an operational property of the production line. 37. A production line comprising: an extrusion station; a product processing station; and a thermal imaging-based monitoring system comprising: a first imaging sensor operative at a wavelength in the range between about 8 pm - about 14 pm, wherein the first imaging sensor is positioned at or after the product processing station, wherein the first imaging sensor is configured image a comestible product conveyed on a conveyor belt of the production line at or after exiting the product processing station. 38. The production line of claim 37, further comprising a second imaging sensor configured to enable imaging of the comestible product at another station of the production line and/or at a different wavelength. 39. The system of claim 38, wherein the other station is the extrusion station. 40. The system of claim 38, wherein the different wavelength is a wavelength in the visible or UV spectrum.
